stimulus.content: Congress passes a new bill and sends it to the President. The President does not agree with the bill and vetoes it. Congress still wants the bill to become a law.

stem: What can Congress do to pass the bill after a presidential veto?

choices.text: Ask the Supreme Court to sign it
Vote again with a two-thirds majority
Hold a national vote by all citizens
Send the bill to the state governors

answer_explanation: Congress can override a presidential veto by voting again with a two-thirds majority in both the House and Senate. The Supreme Court cannot sign bills into law. National citizen votes and state governors are not part of this process.
